mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or signal loses predictive power with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which increases corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant during mid-cycle expansion, with Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over 10-year out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product confirm sustained economic growth, which supports corporate earnings trajectories and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth correlates with negative forward returns in > 60% of observations, or rolling Rank IC falls below -0.01 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.4 percent signals accelerating price pressures that force monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 70% of high-inflation trigger months, or inflation threshold fails to predict return direction with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and retail sales demonstrates synchronized labor market strength and consumer spending power, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is dominated by single-series noise in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates exceeding 5.5 percent indicate structural labor market weakness that suppresses corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Returns are positive in > 65% of instances when unemployment crosses the threshold, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance over 5 years.
